_note: Batch 2: verified real programs gathered via web research on 2026-07-06, targeting categories underrepresented in batch 1 (art/music, medical/nursing, environmental/agricultural, teen/high-school academic-year, and denominations beyond Modern Orthodox: Conservative, Reconstructionist, Chabad). Where a cost, contact detail, or other field could not be confirmed from a current source, the field says so explicitly instead of guessing -- see inline '(unconfirmed...)' notes in cost/description/contact fields and treat those as needing a human follow-up before being presented as fact. durationType is one of TEN_DAY|SUMMER|SEMESTER|GAP_YEAR|CUSTOM.

arts_and_music.name: Rimon School of Music - Study Abroad Program
arts_and_music.organization: Rimon School of Jazz and Contemporary Music
arts_and_music.location: Ramat HaSharon (commuter campus, near Tel Aviv)
arts_and_music.description: A one-year, English-language program for international students at Israel's leading contemporary music school, covering music theory, ear training, arranging, music technology, songwriting, composition, improvisation, private instruction, and ensembles. Begins with a month-long Hebrew ulpan and orientation. Students fluent in Hebrew may continue into Rimon's full majors (jazz, performance, production, composition, education). Commuter campus -- no on-site housing, but staff help arrange shared apartments.
arts_and_music.durationText: 1 academic year (November-July); Hebrew-fluent students may extend
arts_and_music.cost: (unconfirmed - not published online) contact admissions for current tuition
arts_and_music.signupInstructions: Apply via videotaped audition, personal interview, and online musical assessment.

arts_and_music.name: Bezalel Academy of Arts and Design - Student Exchange Program
arts_and_music.organization: Bezalel Academy of Arts and Design
arts_and_music.description: One or two semesters of study at Israel's oldest and most prestigious art and design academy, in fields including fine arts, photography, visual communication, animation, fashion/jewelry design, ceramics and glass, industrial design, architecture, and material culture. Courses in Hebrew or English; a week-long Hebrew course precedes the spring semester. Exchange students are housed at Hebrew University dormitories. IMPORTANT CAVEAT: this is a university exchange, not an open-enrollment program -- applicants must be nominated by a partner institution's international office (Bezalel partners with ~100 art schools worldwide), so it is not available to individuals applying directly without a partner-school affiliation.
arts_and_music.durationText: 1-2 semesters; spring semester runs mid-February to end of June
arts_and_music.cost: (unconfirmed) typically home-institution tuition applies under exchange agreements, not separate Bezalel tuition -- confirm with home school's study-abroad office
arts_and_music.signupInstructions: Must be nominated by your home institution's international/study-abroad office, which emails Bezalel's exchange office with your name, department, email, and intended semester.

arts_and_music.name: Artzi
arts_and_music.location: Jerusalem, Tel Aviv, and a kibbutz
arts_and_music.description: A nine-month artistic gap-year program for 18-19 year-olds studying visual arts, music, theater, or dance. Students choose one of four tracks and get weekly one-on-one studio time with professional artists, plus a personal artistic project. Participants live alongside Israeli students from the 'Ruach Nachon' pre-army artistic academy, building Hebrew fluency and Israeli friendships. Includes trips, volunteering, and Jewish learning.
arts_and_music.cost: $26,500 (includes room, board, health insurance, trips, and artistic mentorship)
arts_and_music.signupInstructions: Application requires an interview (sometimes a second interview) and a portfolio submission (visual art, music, photography, or dance) plus a short essay on your interest in the arts and in a gap year.

arts_and_music.name: Nesiya Kehillah Summer Program
arts_and_music.location: Jerusalem, with touring through the Negev, Galilee, Tel Aviv, and the Judean Desert
arts_and_music.description: A pluralistic, non-movement-affiliated program combining outdoor adventure with workshops in visual, performing, and literary arts, community service, and creative Jewish study. Israeli and North American participants travel together through four regions of Israel exploring the land's role in Jewish history and the future of Jewish life, with a multi-day service project benefiting Israeli children.
arts_and_music.cost: (unconfirmed - not published online) contact Nesiya directly
arts_and_music.signupInstructions: Contact Nesiya directly for current application details; see website for program dates.

medical_and_nursing.name: Young Judaea Year Course - Medical Track
medical_and_nursing.location: Based in Jerusalem and Tel Aviv, with placements nationwide
medical_and_nursing.description: The largest gap-year program in Israel (9 months, up to 30 college credits) offers a Medical Track: a 10-day intensive course followed by five weeks of hands-on volunteering aboard Magen David Adom ambulances alongside Israeli paramedics, plus tours and workshops with Israeli medical staff. Eligible for 3 college credits. No prior medical experience required, but a conversational Hebrew oral test must be passed before volunteering with MDA.
medical_and_nursing.durationText: 9 months overall (Medical Track component: 10-day course + 5 weeks of MDA volunteering)
medical_and_nursing.cost: (unconfirmed exact current sticker price) 2026-27 payment-plan minimum after scholarships/discounts is reported at $16,000; confirm full tuition with Young Judaea
medical_and_nursing.signupInstructions: Apply online; rolling admissions until July 1 each year, though earlier applications get priority for section/placement requests.

medical_and_nursing.name: Medical School for International Health (MSIH)
medical_and_nursing.organization: Ben-Gurion University of the Negev / Columbia University Irving Medical Center
medical_and_nursing.description: A four-year, North American-style M.D. program taught in English, integrating global health coursework throughout. First three years in Israel (with Medical Hebrew in years 1-2), fourth-year clinical electives at Columbia University Medical Center, and an eight-week clerkship in an underserved community worldwide. Applicants need an undergraduate degree, at least one year of North American college coursework, and an MCAT or GAMSAT score; must hold a passport from a country with diplomatic relations with Israel.
medical_and_nursing.cost: (unconfirmed - not published in research) contact admissions office
medical_and_nursing.signupInstructions: Apply through the MSIH admissions office (New York-based admissions team); requires MCAT or GAMSAT.

medical_and_nursing.name: Tel Aviv University NY State/American Program (Sackler / Gray Faculty of Medicine)
medical_and_nursing.organization: Tel Aviv University, Gray Faculty of Medical and Health Sciences
medical_and_nursing.description: An English-language, four-year M.D. program chartered by the Regents of the University of the State of New York, established in 1976, open to U.S. and Canadian citizens/permanent residents. Requires a bachelor's degree, at least one year of North American college coursework, and the MCAT; some Hebrew competency required for clinical years.
medical_and_nursing.cost: (unconfirmed - not published in research) contact admissions office
medical_and_nursing.signupInstructions: Apply through the NY State/American Program admissions office; MCAT required.

medical_and_nursing.name: APF Medical & Nursing Volunteer Program
medical_and_nursing.organization: American Physicians and Friends for Medicine in Israel (APF)
medical_and_nursing.location: Hospitals and community/primary-care clinics throughout Israel
medical_and_nursing.description: Places North American medical students, nursing students, and other healthcare professionals as volunteers in Israeli hospitals and primary-care services, matched to placements by ability and availability. Also runs an annual APF/Birthright medical trip for medical, nursing, and allied-health students to see Israeli healthcare firsthand.
medical_and_nursing.durationText: As short as 2 weeks, up to 6+ weeks for longer placements
medical_and_nursing.cost: (unconfirmed) likely participant covers own travel/lodging as a volunteer placement -- confirm with APF
medical_and_nursing.signupInstructions: Contact APF directly by phone or email to discuss placement options and the annual Birthright medical trip.

environmental_and_agricultural.name: Arava Institute for Environmental Studies
environmental_and_agricultural.description: An accredited university-level semester (or full-year) environmental studies program bringing together Israeli, Palestinian, Jordanian, and international students to study topics like sustainable agriculture, cross-border water management, and environmental diplomacy. Fee includes tuition, room, full kibbutz-dining-room board, health insurance, and field trips. Merit-based funding applications due April 15 (fall) / November 15 (spring); full scholarships available for Arab Israeli, Palestinian, and Jordanian students.
environmental_and_agricultural.cost: $10,000/semester for North American students (includes tuition, room, board, insurance, field trips)
environmental_and_agricultural.signupInstructions: Apply online; email the Admissions Coordinator for visa/application questions.

environmental_and_agricultural.name: Kibbutz Lotan Green Apprenticeship
environmental_and_agricultural.organization: Kibbutz Lotan Center for Creative Ecology
environmental_and_agricultural.description: A month-long, hands-on program in permaculture, natural building (straw-bale and earth-plaster construction), and organic agriculture, living on Lotan's eco-campus of student-built earthen domes. Graduates receive an internationally recognized Permaculture Design Certificate.
environmental_and_agricultural.cost: $3,500 (includes a $175 registration fee, all food and accommodation)
environmental_and_agricultural.signupInstructions: Apply via the Kibbutz Lotan website; contact for current session dates.

environmental_and_agricultural.name: Eco-Israel Program
environmental_and_agricultural.location: Hava & Adam Farm, between Jerusalem and Tel Aviv (near Modi'in)
environmental_and_agricultural.description: A 5-month permaculture and sustainable-living program for English-speaking young adults ages 18-24, centered on a Permaculture Design Course (internationally certified) and hands-on farm work. A Masa-affiliated long-term program, so Masa grants/scholarships ($1,000-$6,000) can offset the cost; the farm also offers additional needs-based scholarships.
environmental_and_agricultural.cost: $14,000 total (housing, food, classes, field trips) before Masa grants/scholarships
environmental_and_agricultural.signupInstructions: Schedule a video-call interview with the Eco-Israel Coordinator by email.

teen_and_highschool_academic_year.name: Alexander Muss High School in Israel (AMHSI)
teen_and_highschool_academic_year.location: Hod HaSharon (main campus, 20 minutes from Tel Aviv); also Be'er Sheva
teen_and_highschool_academic_year.description: A pluralistic, fully-accredited study-abroad program for high schoolers combining general studies with experiential Israel-studies curriculum (learning history on-site at the actual locations). Offered as a semester, a shorter 'Mini-Mester', or summer sessions; students can earn college credit through the University of Miami. IMPORTANT NOTE: the Reform movement's own standalone semester program, URJ Heller High (formerly NFTY-EIE, previously based at Kibbutz Tzuba), recently merged into AMHSI as of a recent year due to declining enrollment -- Heller High students now live and take Jewish studies together as a track within the AMHSI campus near Tel Aviv rather than as a separate program. Confirm current Heller-track availability/dates directly with AMHSI since this transition is recent.
teen_and_highschool_academic_year.cost: (unconfirmed - not published in research) need-based financial aid, scholarships, and payment plans are offered
teen_and_highschool_academic_year.signupInstructions: Apply online ('Apply Now') or attend an information session; see website for session dates.

underrepresented_denominations.name: Nativ Track (Aardvark Israel)
underrepresented_denominations.organization: United Synagogue of Conservative/Masorti Judaism (USCJ) in partnership with Aardvark Israel
underrepresented_denominations.location: Jerusalem and Tel Aviv (one semester each)
underrepresented_denominations.description: The Conservative/Masorti movement's gap-year program, run in partnership with Aardvark Israel: a full academic year split between Jerusalem and Tel Aviv, with intensive Hebrew ulpan, university-accredited coursework, internships/volunteering, and leadership training grounded in Conservative/Masorti values. IMPORTANT FLAG: USCJ suspended its previous standalone Nativ program in December 2023 citing budget and recruitment challenges, and relaunched it via this new Aardvark partnership starting September 2026 -- since this is a brand-new relaunch, confirm current logistics, dates, and costs directly before publishing as final, as details may still be settling.
underrepresented_denominations.durationText: Full academic year, one semester each in Jerusalem and Tel Aviv
underrepresented_denominations.cost: ~$29,000 (excludes flights and personal spending money; needs-based USCJ scholarships available)
underrepresented_denominations.signupInstructions: Early applications due December 1 (decisions by January 10); regular applications due February 1 (decisions by March 15).

underrepresented_denominations.name: No'ar Hadash Israel Program
underrepresented_denominations.location: Multiple regions across Israel (touring), based from Jerusalem
underrepresented_denominations.description: The Reconstructionist movement's Israel program for 11th graders: after building community together at Camp JRF, participants travel to Israel for a month, tracing how Jews have lived on the land throughout history, spending Shabbat in Jerusalem, watching sunrise over Masada, doing tikkun olam projects with Israeli peers, and meeting Reconstructionist rabbis living in Israel.
underrepresented_denominations.cost: (unconfirmed - not published in research) financial assistance available for some participants -- contact Camp JRF
underrepresented_denominations.signupInstructions: Contact Camp JRF / No'ar Hadash directly for current application details.

underrepresented_denominations.name: Mayanot Post-High-School Program (Men)
underrepresented_denominations.location: Jerusalem (Sderot Ben Tsvi 7, near the shuk and Old City)
underrepresented_denominations.description: A Chabad-Chassidic yeshiva program for male high-school graduates ages 18-19, offering Torah study with a Chassidic approach in a dedicated Post High School track on Mayanot's Men's Campus. No prior Hebrew, Jewish studies background, or religious commitment required for acceptance. Runs a full academic year (with a 3-week Pesach break) but students may also come for just a semester or as little as 3 weeks.
underrepresented_denominations.durationText: Full year (Sept-June) recommended; semester or as short as 3 weeks also possible
underrepresented_denominations.cost: (unconfirmed - see Mayanot's tuition PDF) contact admissions for current figures
underrepresented_denominations.signupInstructions: Apply online; applications due no later than two weeks before each program start date.

underrepresented_denominations.name: Machon Alte - Seminary Chaya Mushka
underrepresented_denominations.location: Tzfat (Safed), off Jerusalem Street in the city center
underrepresented_denominations.description: The only Chabad-Lubavitch women's school in Israel, offering full-time Jewish studies (Jewish law, Torah and Prophets, Jewish meditation and prayer, Chassidic philosophy, Hebrew ulpan) for women of any age or background, on a year, semester, or short-term basis. Dormitory housing on premises, 2 students per room with a private bathroom.
underrepresented_denominations.durationText: 8-10 months typical; semester and short-term options available
underrepresented_denominations.cost: (unconfirmed - not published in research) scholarships available -- contact seminary directly
underrepresented_denominations.signupInstructions: Apply via Machon Alte's website or through Masa Israel's program listing.
